summary: SPH4U modern-physics problems on the photoelectric effect treat light as photons of energy E = hf. An electron is ejected only if hf exceeds the work function W, and the surplus is its kinetic energy: KE_max = hf − W.
method: Find the photon energy E = hf from the light's frequency.
Compare it with the metal's work function W.
If hf < W, no electron is ejected.
If hf > W, the maximum kinetic energy is KE_max = hf − W.
The threshold frequency that just frees an electron is f₀ = W/h.

example.title: Kinetic energy of a photoelectron
example.find: the maximum kinetic energy of an ejected electron
steps: E > W, so an electron is ejected.
